title: Vana Dorja
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Vana}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image= |name=Vana Dorja |homeworld= |birth=c. [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'', describes Vana Dorja as "a [[Gender/Legends|woman]] of about thirty." As the novel is set in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]], per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', Dorja's [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]] [[Standard year/Legends|year]] would be approximately [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], thirty years prior. |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=Dark |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents=[[Dorja]]{{CSWECite|I|200|Dorja, Commander Vana}} |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Imperial Remnant]] **[[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|To voluntarily relinquish power—I confess I don't understand it. In the Empire, we are taught not to decline responsibility once it is given to us.|Vana Dorja|}} '''Vana Dorja''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who served as a [[Commander/Legends|commander]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Navy]] of the [[Imperial Remnant]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. She was an inquisitive and observant woman with a partisan Imperial frame of mind. Her [[Dorja family|family]] had a long history of naval service to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], with her [[Dorja|father]] having [[Captain/Legends|captained]] an [[Relentless (Dorja)|Imperial Star Destroyer]] into the [[Post-Imperial era/Legends|era]] of the Remnant. Although the Remnant's [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]] chose not to participate in the war, Dorja was sent on an allegedly commercial mission to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where she witnessed the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|attack]] the world in [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]]. She escaped before the invaders conquered the capital, and in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]], she made her way to the New Republic's provisional capital planet of [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] and requested a ride back to [[Imperial Space]]. Dorja was shuttled home by Captain [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and the [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] aboard their [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], as the [[Solo family/Legends|Solos]] were themselves on a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] mission to the Remnant. Organa Solo suspected that Dorja had been serving as an Imperial [[Spy/Legends|spy]] on Coruscant and was frequently unsettled by the commander's presence, but when the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] was interdicted from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] by a Yuuzhan Vong [[dovin basal]], Dorja crewed one of the ''Millennium Falcon''{{'s}} [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser]] [[Turret/Legends|turrets]] and helped [[Skirmish on the Hydian Way|destroy]] Yuuzhan Vong [[Yorik-et|coralskipper]] [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]]. Their victory was aided by the arrival of a force of New Republic starfighters, whose leader, [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Jagged Fel]], came aboard the ''Millennium Falcon'' and discussed with Dorja what he thought the Remnant's best interests would be as the Yuuzhan Vong War continued to rage. After Fel departed, the freighter continued to Imperial [[Realspace/Legends|Space]], and Dorja quietly slipped away from the Solos when they landed aboard a [[Unidentified Super Star Destroyer|Super Star Destroyer]] at the Remnant's capital of [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]]. ==Biography== ===Escape from the capital=== {{Quote|I thank you again for your offer of transit to the Empire. It was fortunate that you have business there.''"
"''Very fortunate.|Vana Dorja and Leia Organa Solo|}} The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] Vana Dorja was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. Her [[Parent/Legends|father]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Dorja]], was an [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], continuing their wealthy [[Dorja family|family's]]{{CSWECite|I|200|Dorja, Captain}} rich tradition of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] and [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republican]] military service—the preceding six generations of Dorja men had all been [[Navy/Legends|naval]] [[Officer/Legends|officers]]. Vana Dorja's father became the captain of the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Relentless (Dorja)|''Relentless'']]''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]'' and he continued to command the [[Warship/Legends|warship]] into the [[Post-Imperial era/Legends|era]] of the [[Imperial Remnant]]. The younger Dorja followed in her father's footsteps and became a [[Commander/Legends|commander]] in the Remnant's Navy. In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invaded]] [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Although the Imperial Remnant's [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]] elected to sit out the war and fortify [[Imperial Space]] while the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] dealt with the invaders,''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant]]'' Dorja was sent to the New Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], allegedly to negotiate a commercial treaty and to purchase [[Ulban Arms/Legends|Ulban]] [[Droid brain/Legends|droid brains]] for Imperial [[Hydroponics/Legends|hydroponics]] [[Farm/Legends|farms]]. The negotiations failed due to the fact that the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Brain/Legends|brains]] could also be used for military purposes, and in [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]], during Dorja's extended stay on-world, the Yuuzhan Vong [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|attacked]] Coruscant. Dorja escaped off-world before the capital was conquered, and in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]] she arrived at the New Republic's provisional capital planet of [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] and asked for a ride back to Imperial [[Realspace/Legends|Space]]. [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], an [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]] attached to the New Republic, was about to embark on a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] mission to the Remnant and agreed to bring Dorja aboard her [[Marriage/Legends|husband's]] [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. ===Hitching a ride=== {{Dialogue|Solo|We have about three standard minutes to contact with the enemy. Fire dead ahead, on my mark.|Dorja|Dead ahead? How unorthodox… have you considered maneuver?|Organa Solo|Don't second-guess the pilot! Keep this channel clear unless you have something of value to say!|Dorja|Apologies.|attr=Han Solo, Vana Dorja, and Leia Organa Solo, while taking on a group of Yuuzhan Vong coralskippers|src=}} [[File:Rebel_Stand_art.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Vana Dorja rode back to Imperial Space aboard Han and Leia Organa Solo's ''Millennium Falcon''.]] Organa Solo was suspicious about Dorja, suspecting that she was a commercial [[Spy/Legends|spy]] who had been on Coruscant to witness its fall. She also believed that Dorja's negotiations had been intended to go nowhere and that her subsequent escape had been planned long in advance. Accordingly, Organa Solo declared certain areas of the ''Millennium Falcon'' off-limits to Dorja and scanned the commander for any technological secrets. The result was negative, and the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] embarked on its journey. While they were traveling through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], Dorja made conversation with the [[Solo family/Legends|Solos']] [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], and later dined with Organa Solo and her husband [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] in the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] main hold. They spoke of power and of leadership, and Dorja mused out loud how she thought the Empire would have dealt with the Yuuzhan Vong threat had [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] still ruled the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. The conversation was interrupted by the wail of [[Klaxon/Legends|alarms]], however, as the ship was interdicted from [[Lightspeed/Legends|lightspeed]] by a Yuuzhan Vong [[dovin basal]] in an area of space along the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]]. Captain Solo rushed to the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] while Dorja and Organa Solo took to the freighter's [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannons]], Dorja in the dorsal [[Turret/Legends|turret]]. Solo took the ''Millennium Falcon'' directly toward the dovin basal, the [[Gravity/Legends|gravitic pull]] of which hid them from the [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] of six Yuuzhan Vong [[Yorik-et|coralskipper]] [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] that were positioned on its other side. When he swept past at the last [[Standard minute/Legends|minute]], he fired [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]], which, combined with Dorja and Organa Solo's [[Laser/Legends|laser]] fire, [[Skirmish on the Hydian Way|destroyed]] four and damaged one of the coralskippers. Six others were pursuing from behind, which led to Solo taking the freighter around for another pass inside the dovin basal's gravitic pull and destroying one of the pursuers. When the lone survivor of the first [[Flight group/Legends|flight]] caught up to them, Dorja destroyed it. Solo then halted his [[Solo Slingshot|slingshot]] around the dovin basal in order to confuse the remaining enemy [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], which allowed Dorja and Organa Solo to destroy another coralskipper and damage its [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmate]]. They finished off one more as Solo rocketed back out of the dovin basal's gravity, but before they could tangle with their remaining foes, a joint force of New Republic [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|E-wings]] and [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Nssis-class Clawcraft|clawcraft]] decanted from hyperspace and took care of the remaining coralskippers. Solo invited [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Jagged Fel]], the force's leader and [[Jaina Solo Fel|his daughter's]] boyfriend, aboard the ''Millennium Falcon'' for dinner. After Fel was introduced to Dorja, he joined the others at the table in the main hold. When Organa Solo mentioned her diplomatic mission to the Imperial Remnant, Fel wished her luck but mentioned that it was in the Remnant's short-term interests to ally themselves with the Yuuzhan Vong. An intrigued Dorja bid him to continue, and his musings that the Yuuzhan Vong would offer vast amounts of territory to its Imperial allies had Dorja's attention until he pointed out that in the long term, the invaders would end up betraying and destroying the Remnant. He departed soon afterward, and the ''Millennium Falcon'' continued on its long voyage through Yuuzhan Vong territory. When they arrived in the [[Sartinaynian system]] at the Imperial capital world of [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]], they landed in a [[Unidentified Super Star Destroyer|Super Star Destroyer's]] [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]], where Dorja quietly slipped away. As the Solos were escorted to [[Imperial Headquarters]] on the [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world's]] surface, Organa Solo suspected that Dorja was briefing the Remnant's [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]], [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], on what she had learned about the state of the New Republic. The Solos then met with Pellaeon and successfully negotiated an exchange of information: in return for all of the intelligence that the New Republic had on the Yuuzhan Vong, Pellaeon provided the Remnant's secret [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace routes]] through the galaxy's [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I can't help but wonder how the old Empire would have handled the crisis. I hope you will forgive my partisan attitude, but it seems to me that the Emperor would have mobilized his entire armament at the first threat, and dealt with the Yuuzhan Vong in an efficient and expeditious manner, through the use of overwhelming force. Certainly better than Borsk Fey'lya's policy—if I understood it correctly as a policy—of negotiating with the invaders at the same time as he was fighting them, sending signals of weakness to a ruthless enemy who used negotiation only as a cover for further conquests.|Dorja, to Organa Solo|}} Vana Dorja had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and bobbed dark [[Hair/Legends|hair]] that she tucked neatly into her [[Imperial military uniforms/Legends|Imperial uniform]]. She was an observant, inquisitive woman with the bland and pleasant face of a diplomat—attributes that Leia Organa Solo thought were appropriate for a probable Imperial spy. Organa Solo found her manner of speaking to also be bland, and although Dorja was polite and accommodating to her hosts aboard the ''Millennium Falcon'', she created unsettling moments for them quite easily. Dorja appreciated the efficiency of C-3PO but found him to be slightly talkative. During the freighter's [[Space warfare/Legends|battle]] with Yuuzhan Vong coralskippers, Dorja thought that Solo's creative piloting was highly unorthodox, but she nevertheless adjusted to it and proved herself a capable [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]]. Her usual restraint was broken when she whooped with joy after destroying an enemy ship. Dorja held a partisan view toward the Empire, believing that [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine would have immediately crushed the Yuuzhan Vong with his [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial war machine]]. She felt that New Republic [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Borsk Fey'lya]]'s method of negotiating with the Yuuzhan Vong while simultaneously fighting them had been a weak and hesitant method that had in turn led to chaos. Dorja listened intently when she believed that Jagged Fel was advocating that the Imperial Remnant ally itself with the invaders rather than with the New Republic, and she was also critical of the New Republic's lack of a clear chain of command following Fey'lya's [[Death/Legends|death]]. She suspected that Organa Solo, a former Chief of State herself, would attempt to return to power—to Dorja, the voluntary relinquishing of power was tantamount to declining one's responsibility. Han Solo was openly critical of Imperial methods, and after an angry tirade of his about past failures of the Empire, Organa Solo thought that she detected amusement in Dorja's eyes. ==Behind the scenes== Vana Dorja plays a minor role in [[Walter Jon Williams]]'s ''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' ([[2002]]), the fourteenth novel in [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series. Although the book only vaguely discusses her parentage, merely calling her "the daughter of the captain of a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]]," [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' explicitly identifies her father as Captain Dorja, a character who first appeared in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s [[1992]] novel ''[[Dark Force Rising]]''.''[[Dark Force Rising]]'' ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Vana Dorja |es=Vana Dorja }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Dorja, Vana}} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy officers of the Imperial Remnant]]
